---
title: "quarto-dbml Example"
format:
  html: default
  pdf: default
filters:
  - quarto-dbml
# Uncomment to force a theme for all diagrams in this document:
# dbml:
#   theme: dark
---

## Default (auto — follows system/browser preference)

```{.dbml}
Table users {
  id integer [pk, increment]
  username varchar [not null, unique]
  email varchar [not null]
  created_at timestamp
}

Table orders {
  id integer [pk, increment]
  user_id integer [not null]
  total decimal
  status varchar
  created_at timestamp
}

Table order_items {
  id integer [pk, increment]
  order_id integer [not null]
  product_id integer [not null]
  quantity integer
  price decimal
}

Table products {
  id integer [pk, increment]
  name varchar [not null]
  description text
  price decimal
  stock integer
}

Ref: orders.user_id > users.id
Ref: order_items.order_id > orders.id
Ref: order_items.product_id > products.id
```

## Forced light (block-level override)

```{.dbml theme="light"}
Table categories {
  id integer [pk]
  name varchar [not null]
  parent_id integer
}

Table products {
  id integer [pk]
  category_id integer
  name varchar [not null]
  price decimal
}

Ref: products.category_id > categories.id
```

## Forced dark (block-level override)

```{.dbml theme="dark"}
Table sessions {
  id varchar [pk]
  user_id integer [not null]
  expires_at timestamp [not null]
  created_at timestamp
}

Table users {
  id integer [pk]
  email varchar [not null, unique]
}

Ref: sessions.user_id > users.id
```
